info.title: http2mac — MAC Address / OUI Lookup API
info.description:
Resolves one or more MAC addresses against the IEEE OUI database.
The internal database is built automatically from the letstool CDN () and refreshed every 4 hours.

Each MAC address is validated, classified as Unicast or Multicast, identified as UAA (Universally Administered) or LAA (Locally Administered), and enriched with the registered organisation name, address, country code, assignment type (MA-L, MA-M, MA-S, CID, IAB), block range, and virtualisation hint.

post.summary: Look up one or more MAC addresses against the OUI database
post.description:
Accepts a **single MAC** (`mac`) or a **batch** (`macs`). Returns validation status, unicast/multicast classification, IEEE registration status, and full OUI metadata for each queried MAC.

Exactly one of `mac` or `macs` must be provided per request.
Maximum batch size is controlled by `MAC_MAX_MACS` / `-max-macs` (default 100).

**Accepted MAC formats:**
- `xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx` (Linux colon-separated)
- `xx-xx-xx-xx-xx-xx` (Windows hyphen-separated)
- `xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx` (dot-separated per-byte)
- `xxxx.xxxx.xxxx` (Cisco three-group)
- `xxxxxxxxxxxx` (no separator, 12 hex digits)

All formats are normalised to lowercase `xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx` in the response.

405.description: Method not allowed — only POST is accepted

get.summary: Download the current mac.mmdb database file
get.description: Serves the current `mac.mmdb` binary for peer instances that want to synchronise without fetching from the CDN.
Browser-based interface for single and batch MAC address OUI lookup. Supports dark/light themes and 15 languages.

MACRequest.description: Exactly one of `mac` or `macs` must be provided.

mac.description: Single MAC address to look up. Accepted formats: `xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx`, `xx-xx-xx-xx-xx-xx`, `xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx`, `xxxx.xxxx.xxxx`, `xxxxxxxxxxxx`.

macs.description: Batch of MAC addresses to look up. Maximum count is controlled by `MAC_MAX_MACS` / `-max-macs` (default 100).

status.description:
- `SUCCESS`: at least one MAC was found in the OUI database; `answers` is fully populated.
- `NOTFOUND`: no MACs matched any OUI block; `answers` still contains validation and type data.
- `ERROR`: malformed request (neither `mac` nor `macs`, or both), batch too large, or database not yet initialised.

answers.description: One entry per queried MAC address. Always present for valid requests; each entry contains at minimum `mac`, `valid`, `type`, and `registered`.

mac.description: The normalised MAC address in lowercase colon-separated format (`xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx`). For invalid inputs, the original input string is echoed back.

valid.description: `true` if the MAC address passed format validation (exactly 6 bytes, valid hex characters, supported separator pattern).

type.description: Address type based on the LSB of the first octet: `Unicast` (bit=0), `Multicast` (bit=1, includes broadcast). `Unknown` is returned for invalid MACs.

admin_type.description: Administration type based on the U/L bit (bit 1 of the first octet): UAA (Universally Administered Address — globally unique, factory-assigned by the manufacturer) or LAA (Locally Administered Address — manually/locally assigned, often random or virtual). Only present when valid is true.

registered.description: `true` if the MAC address was found in the IEEE OUI database with an assignment type of MA-L, MA-M, MA-S, CID, or IAB.

oui.description: The OUI prefix as stored in the IEEE database (e.g. `00:11:22`).

organisation_name.description: The organisation name registered for this OUI block.

organization_address.description: The postal address of the registered organisation.

address_min.description: The lowest MAC address in the registered block (e.g. `00:11:22:00:00:00`).

address_max.description: The highest MAC address in the registered block (e.g. `00:11:22:ff:ff:ff`).

block_size.description: Number of MAC addresses in the registered block. MA-L = 16,777,216; MA-M = 1,048,576; MA-S = 4,096.

assignment.description: IEEE assignment type: MA-L (24-bit OUI), MA-M (28-bit), MA-S (36-bit), CID (Company ID), IAB (Individual Address Block).

virtual.description: Virtualisation hint from the database. `"False"` means the block is not known to be used by a virtualisation platform. Any other value names the virtualisation technology (e.g. `"VMware"`, `"VirtualBox"`).
